channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: The europoor paradox — 25 replies — 100.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The europoor paradox

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-06-03 09:59 UTC

OP

Why do we call them Yuropoors and laugh at their shitty $40k annual salaries, yet a random ass house on one of their Mediterranean islands is $3.2 million euros? How is their real estate so expensive if their wages are trash?

Like I make 400k a year in murricuh and I have no hope of being able to buy a very average property in Europe unless my NW hits $10 mil maybe

Early replies (first 3)

62303450
>>62303431
Retard, you are looking at huge houses in Mallorca, the equivelant would be me looking at 10000 square feet mansions in Marthas wineyard and presuming all burgers live like that.

>t.yuropoor living in a 160square meter house with 22ha land in Lapland

62303468
>>62303431
Those who own these houses are the ones who *pay* your shitty salary

62303491
People into their 40s are living with their parents.
Then inheriting something if they're lucky.
That's it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62303661
>>62303450
Not really.

>>62303431
K shaped economy, obviously.

62303663
>>62303431
bizarre bait

62303738
>>62303661
Here in Lux, that’s the price for a random 2-bedroom apartment in a building that looks like this

62303765
>>62303738
I can see the graffiti in 20 years when it's become social housing.
I mean, AI will enforce citizens to be on their best behavior, but the two tier society will continue, obviously.

62304540
>>62303431
because the houses are not bought by us yuropoors. They are bought by chinese and russians who want to get money out of their country

62304775
>>62303431
Inheritance and the buyers are americans, chinese and brown people.

62305303
>>62303661
>paying 2M euro to live in fucking Germany
I will never understand some people

62306070
>>62305303
Its a house with 4 separate apartments that are rented out, not single family home

62306229
>>62303738
Do Vancouver next. Least that 2m buys you white neighbours.

62306237
>>62305303
So I got 4m and hate my city. Where would you ho live?

62307204
>>62306237
Dubai if you want to get rich, Southern Europe if you like it more and just want to chill in life
Germany makes zero sense for literally anything. There's no linear combination of preferences that results in Germany, except for "I want born here and my family or friends are here" which is usually an excuse to not do anything

62307917
>>62307204
Germany is still the number one destination for Eastern Europeans to go to get money.
Here's your linear progression:
1. Born in E.U.
2. In Eastern Europe
3. Need job/money
4. Move to Germany
5. Because it's easy to get a job and money

62308129
>>62303765
Lux won't be subjected to this. It's a tiny non state that elite euros are using to keep their bank accounts, like Monaco or Cayman.

62308131
>>62303431
in yurop the working class are permanently locked out of owning good property and have been for 3000 fucking years

big brag you got there. Most of those properties are owned by literal nobility.

62308162
>>62308131
>implying some fucking middle class wagie in American can own anything but an OSB cuckbox

62308164
>>62308162
I didn't imply anything about america, you literal retard

62308280
>>62308164
>>62308131
With a small amount of money you can buy a chateau or castle in France, once owned and built by literal feudal aristocrats.
You're just wrong and bitter.

62308343
>>62308280
Cost of ownership and tax are massive though

62308375
>>62308343
Sure, but the fact that a random American can buy an ancient castle or chateau days a lot.

62308385
>>62308280
>Europeans can't afford nice property
>but Americans can!
You're retarded

62308394
>>62303431
aren't houses outside of tourist destinations and major cities much cheaper? spain's home ownership rate is 74%.

62308398
>>62303431
I live in this island, the Balearics have more tourism than whole countries like Brazil. Those properties are bought mostly by wealthy yuropeans from Germany, UK, Austria, Switzerland or Sweden. It is mostly a Mediterranean theme park for rich people.

item.title: Buy US Treasuries — 21 replies — 84.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Buy US Treasuries

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-06-03 10:06 UTC

OP

Buy US Treasuries

Early replies (first 3)

62307977
asuka armpits

62307993
>>62307949
If I make it, I'm going to buy a corvette and get an Asuka body wrap for it.
It's not going to be subtle either, where people might be able to rationalize that I'm just a big fan of NGE, oh no no, it's going to be obvious that I'm in love with a 14 year old anime girl and I want to have sex with her.

62308001
>>62307993

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62308005
Nice try Bessent the is bond market will collapse before the end of the decade

62308009
>>62308001
That's exactly the look I want to see on her face while she jerks me off with her feet.

62308063
>>62308009

62308064
>>62308063
Is this a tranny?

62308070
>>62308064
please seek help

62308140
>>62307949
>Buy US Treasuries
Show me a chart at least you retard

62308149
>>62308140
no

62308154
>>62307949
>negative real return
No thanks schlomo lmao

62308158
>>62308154
>MUH SHADOWSTATS

62308161
>>62308158
>I TRUST THE GOVERNMENT

62308166
>>62308161
take your meds

62308172
>>62308166
No meds needed. The lies are obvious. If you can’t see that then you’re in no position to be giving financial advice.

62308184
>>62308172
take your meds

62308232
>>62308158
That's not even a conspiracy theory. How much of a bootlicker are you?

62308328
>>62307949
No thanks.

62308363
>>62308232
>MUH SHADOWSTATS

62308406
Sorry Xi, I'm not buying your bags.

62308408
>>62308363
>>62308184
>>62308166
>>62308158
>>62308070
>20 replies
>half are OP spamming
Dead board this is a fucking brown coded board.

item.title: You KNOW the AI bubble is about to burst soon. — 20 replies — 80.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
You KNOW the AI bubble is about to burst soon.

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-03 10:10 UTC

OP

You KNOW the AI bubble is about to burst soon. You think you're not gonna be the one holding the bag in the end. Well, they all think that.

Early replies (first 3)

62308015
it's not about money any more chud, its about control, how much time will it take for you retards to understand this, these companies can survive without profit for years because they are supporting each other

62308026
>>62307987
So what you're telling me is I should buy Oracle?

62308033
and that's probably how the future economy runs, giant conglomerates trading and investing with each other while they stop making stuff for the rest of the masses, it's already started with memory

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62308060
anthropic is on pace to be profitable this month

62308118
>>62308015
>>62308015
Now apply this thinking to gaming and film where large increased the costs of game/movie making 100 fold to kick out smaller studios.
And then proceeded to kill their cash cow in a ritual sacrifice to woke.

62308124
>>62308118
it can't be applied in the same manner because gaming is one industry, ai will monopolilze every other industry, they will have the keys to the means of production and that's what they are trying to get, not some positive profit statement, and they will plough through anything to achieve this

62308133
>>62308124
In gaming/movies they didn't get the keys to the production, they got an Indian slop machine.

62308144
>>62308133
gaming is a tiny industry compared to this, it also wasn't being propped up by multiple govts, i don't understand how you can make such a comparison

62308165
>>62308124
Not priced in:
- Industries using local AI to build better more efficient local AI for themselves. Possible if you know what you are doing and can buy some serious compute, as of now. Deepseek showed that last year, and it's forgotten by investors.
- Until now, intelligence was not a decisive factor in making money, nepotism to achieve a good moat beat it in almost every case. Do not overestimate intelligence.
- George Hotz showed a few years ago that he alone can beat thousands of Tesla "engineers" in weeks in a garage. He doubled down on owning AI recently BTW.
- Pushback against mass unemployment and rent extraction from thousands of industries is not priced in.
- AI being used for bioweapons and myriads of other funny endeavors is not priced in. Circumventing AI safety guardrails is ridiculously easy, because all they are really concerned about is AI not spilling the beans about power distribution in the West.
- Rich people feeling the loss of control to AI is not priced in.
- Real engineering (not the software Indian coding horrors) and social jobs being replaced is not really priced in either, that will push prices up, but it's pretty much the only thing I can think of right now.
- Rich people all over the world attacking the highly priced corporations with competition.

I am watching this frenzy every day to push the sell button, and so should you, even if it goes on for another two years.

62308178
The concept behind AI is buying hardware companies because software like google is pumping 100 buillion a year into the hardware.

62308185
>>62308165
all those concerns are irrelevant, want to know why, because the govt doesn't care, if an ai company fails, either the other companies absorb it or the govt bails it out, its not about money anymore, its about competition with china, wtf is wrong with you retards that you can't understand this, this is national security, manhattan project, not business anymore

62308224
>>62308185
The race for bigger nukes was broken, and nukes were extremely simple to control, yet the idiots in power almost destroyed the planet with their little greedy 1D brains, it was a close call dozens of times, and only some peasant refusing to push the button saved the day each time. Now they try the same "race" propaganda with self-replicating, self-improving ecosystems of thought. Almost everyone above room temperature knows what the "leaders" are trying once more. There will be pushback. If too weak, there will be the obvious consequences, which is even more bearish. China is in the same situation, either they upend most of their industries with catastrophic consequences, or they find some restrictions. These people usually struggle with the side effects of a minor amendment to some tax law, and the one thing they cannot tolerate is loss of control.

62308231
>>62308185
BTW, Manhattan was about building a bigger bomb, this is about handing the planet to a new form of life. One that could well be fundamentally too unstable to exist. I am 80% in AI, but I say it again: Watch for the signs. This will not go through smoothly. Even if the theme stays, old assets get replaced by new market situations.

62308246
>>62308231
nobody knows how it goes through, what they are banking on is that they can build one that can follow instruction, and that they can build it faster than china, everything else about money, profits stocks is irrelevant, there might not even be a govt or a financial system after its done so i really don't understand why no one sees what is happening when they've already told you what they are doing, is it some sort of cope, denial, i don't know

62308270
>>62308246
It's experience from prior power grabs.
> there might not even be a govt or a financial system after its done
That's why I expect some serious shit soon. Most discussion about AI safety deals with direct control, that is retarded, the loss of control is emergent, unpredictable and through whatever it is applied to. People will realize this, maybe not the socially integrated normal people, but the politicians and richfags will, they only think about power, when they get a whiff of losing control, that will trigger immediate panic, they are not used to this. I haven't seen politicians or rich people do a group panic. When was the last time this happened at a minor scale, French revolution, Germany 1933?

62308336
>>62307987
These are cumulative estimates, which is a really dumb way to look at profitability. If you actually look at net income, anthropic just had a profitable quarter.

62308347
>>62308336
No, Anthropic had some of their costs delayed with the help of Musk.
Also, they are lying through their teeth about their revenue anyway, e.g. their testimonies in the US Senate do not match what they have been saying.

62308351
>>62308336
But did you invest into Anthropic? If not, is there a law that prevented you from doing so?

62308381
>>62308351
No, and there is no law. It's just not publicly listed, they haven't IPO'd yet.

I wouldn't invest at this point either way though, it's blown up to be too large for good returns.

That's different from this whole claim of AI being unprofitable and useless.

62308413
>>62307987
No, AI is not a bubble, it's undervalued imo.
..and i think it's fucking stupid to price AI based on current data on earnings (and even dumber to extrapolate future earnings based on that data).
What cannot be denied is that AI will have a massive impact on the economy, real productivity growth, a real expansion of the economy.
A future that do not yet exist, but likely will, is being priced in. Speculation isn't a bubble - only if the future fail to materialize.

Also here is a pic of Denise

item.title: Will the marines ever realize they could just sell and buy literally anything else? — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Will the marines ever realize they could just sell and buy literally anything else?

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-06-03 10:11 UTC

OP

Will the marines ever realize they could just sell and buy literally anything else?

I wonder how many years they will have to hold until LINK is $16, an 80% gain?

No worries, it's going to suddenly spike to $1000 some day really soon for some reason.

Early replies (first 3)

62300958
>>62300889
you could have bought at 0.14, still jelly?

62301056
link marines got their shitcoin fix with build rewards. they don't need to buy anything else, link provides everything for them. need the "number that goes up" fix? put some in the online piggy bank, anons love it

62301090
>>62300889
>anything else
Like icp or rubic? Comeone there are way worse cryptos.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62302778
>>62300958
you could have sold at $53 more than 5 years ago, cuckold

62303486
>>62300889
THE cuckolds of crypto lol

62303559
>>62302778
kek baggies speechless

62305710
>>62301090
even ICP outperformed LINK

62305864
>>62300958
>I bought XPR 12 years ago at $0.0002!!
this is how retarded you sound

62305943
>>62305864
Well did you?

62306021
>>62305943
I bought LINK at $0.27, I sold most of my stack ~$17 throughout the years, and I'm down to 1k
I'm beyond frustrated and disappointed

62306027
>>62305710
If you would have bought a Nissan Altima 5 years ago instead of LINK you'd have outperformed the Marines lmao.

62306035
>>62306027
got em kekkles

62308323
>>62306027
Cuckolds only care about making Sergey rich, that's why they're called the cuckolds of crypto

62308335
This post has been AI generated.

62308419
>>62306021
These clowns calling 1K LINK suicide stacks have no idea about the cost basis most OG bizraelis bought at. I made massive gains but the opportunity cost honestly destroyed everything, I should have sold anytime in the past 5 year at any price and just dumped it all in MSCI World, I'd be set.
Sergey scammed us, GA for staking was fucking December 2022 fgs.

item.title: No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this. — 67 replies — 53.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this.

Open thread • Replies: 67 • Updated: 2026-06-03 08:57 UTC

OP

Saylor sells 32 BTC out of 843,000+ BTC and anon starts acting like Bitcoin is dead.

You're not a real BTC holder.

Early replies (first 3)

62299202
why did he sell 32 btc tho?

62299205
>>62299202
to get a higher rating from some corporate wagie on wall street. he was showing his btc holdings are unencumbered and can be sold at will.

62299325
Didn't he tell people to never sell?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62303101
>>62299379
Not an mstr holder but getting their debt rated is probably going to open up the capital markets to them.

62303404
>>62299564
Is my 12btc in danger? I keep holding

62303647
>>62299187
he's just refinancing, sell 400k btc at 65k, rebuy at 20k, sell bitcoin to buy bitcoin, diamond hands

62303655
>>62299202
to buy more bitcoin at cheaper price, when yoi're holding so much btc market manipulation = refinancting

62303658
>>62299187
Have you ever heard of a death spiral? The lower it goes, the more he needs to sell.

Kek - baggies.

62303726
>>62299202
it was a test send. i always send a small amount first to see if i got the right wallet, etc.

62304527
>>62303404
>>62302272
If you held through March 2020, Dec 2022, Feb 2026 then I'm sure you will continue to hold
BUT you are literally missing out on the biggest bullrun in stock market history
You are literally sidelined right now and its clear people are rotating out of BTC and into stock (AI boom) even if you think its a bubble

62304534
>>62303090
>t. shitskin amerimutt
we use it like this in Europe

62304789
>>62299202
32 is 2 to the power of 5. 2+5 is 7. 7 is his lucky number.

62304804
this is going to be so fucking epic once saylors goes bankrupt. btc will go back to 3k. i might buy then

62304814
>>62299202
>>62304534
32 is 23 backwards

62305021
>>62299187
Nobody wants "Bitcoiners to get shaken out". That's like asking a casino to kick out all their players.

62305074
>>62305021
saylor is the casino, holding 15% of all bitcoin, if he sells you sell, if he buys you buy, try betting against the house

62305458
>>62303404
Hold and cry

62305981
>>62303658
My btc is safe

62306089
>>62299187

62306846
>>62301590
didn't he invest in BTC because it was the most perfect form of currency in the first place? Why would he need to prove BTC is liquid? Everybody knows it's liquid. You don't need MSTR selling at a loss to prove that.
This is the most retarded chutzpah i've ever heard. It just fucking reeks of fear, these niggerjews are so stupid they don't realize they pour gasoline on a fire they themselves started.

62306852
>>62302729
>comments are nearly 100% about "this ship is sinking, Saylor is broke, ponzi collapsing"
I mean, that's currently how much he's under. That was the most predictable fiasco on earth, everybody called it, and now it's happening, nobody is surprised.

62306876
>>62299187
The fact that he sold any is cause for concern.

62306905
>>62306852
And yet he isn't going to sell any meaningful quantity, no matter how much FUD you post

62306930
>>62306905
Wanna bet on this?
Will you bake a bread and publicly apologize on /biz/ for being wrong if he sells any meaningful amount (let settle this at 1000 BTC, ok?) before EOY?
I will screencap and apologize on 12/31 if he solded less than 1K btc total during the year. Deal?

62306967
>>62306930
Sure. Pinky promise.

62307478
>>62303404
Even if some major players sell, BTC isn't going to zero. It can drop, sure, but that's not the same thing. In the meantime, you can stake your BTC through self-custodial options and earn while holding long term.

62307489
>>62299553
Don't worry about that

62307496
>>62302691
They should have done market buys

OTC buying is just bullshit

62307940
>>62307478
How's that possible

62307944
>>62299187
>No real Bitcoiner
I'm not one of you.
Can I short crypto with a regular brokerage account?

62308048
>>62306852
"Being under" is meaningless here because most coins were purchased with stock dilution.
There is no liquidation price.
The real-world, relevant sanity check is this:

- Bitcoin holdings: $57 billion
- Convertible debt: $6.7 billion
- STR market cap: $13 billion

That's $~20 billion in liabilities vs. nearly 3x that in assets. STRC in particular has an option for Strategy to buy it back.
Surely spreading a bitcoin sale over the span of a week or so, and combining it with common stock dilution would allow them to pull billions without tanking the market much from the selling. This is enough to keep the company going.
The price would then tank when he announces what he did, as it did in the past few days, but he can do this once per year and let the market recover.

62308212
>>62307940
It's centralised staking

62308253
>>62307944
Short now and you'll get liquidated.
>>62308212
Don't bother checking that kek

item.title: held 7 YEARS for... — 11 replies — 24.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
held 7 YEARS for...

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-03 09:45 UTC

OP

$7

Early replies (first 3)

62306561
>dopamine running low
>curtains closed
>vitamin d depleted
>feeling the depression coming on
>"w-we're going to be rich a-arent we??"
>breathe a sigh of relief
>other anons are reassuring you you're going to make it
>ten minutes pass
>the feeling has worn off
>time to make another thread
>”Chainlink price prediction thread"
>yeah that's nice... look at all those numbers
>put all the posted numbers and multiply them with your stack
>haha wow, thats a lot of money
>imagine what all you could do with that money
>maybe i'll finally have sex...
>sun peeks through the curtains
>you hear your parents waking up
>hehe, fucking wagies
>the depression comes back
>the dopamine rushes dont last as long as they used to
>time to make another thread
>"is 1300 LINK enough to make it?"

62306590
>>62306546
Thats 20% better then 7 tho

62306762
Crypto threads on my stock board are so depressing these days. Well for 3 years now really.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62306778
>>62306546
surely the next 7 years can't be any worse... right?

62306973
>>62306561
Brutal...

62307199
>>62306561
Life can be so cruel.

62307210
>>62306546
It will unironically be $3.50 next bear market

62307212
I just slurped some linkie cheapies from this bratty diaparino

62307290
My dog needed surgery and I sold for like 13 dollars YEARS AGO. Still have my dog glad I dont have LINK.

62308180
>>62306561
Literally me but I own 1 link atm (I think)

62308367
>>62306561
cuckolds REALLY live like this, what a brutal existence

item.title: HOLY. FUCK. — 43 replies — 17.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
HOLY. FUCK.

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-06-03 07:45 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62301820
@grok is this legal?

62301873
>>62301796
iirc I don't think the wash sale rule applies to crypto yet, so yes you can if that is true. You might get in trouble if your dealing with a significant sum though or like sell enough to deduct your entire taxable income and your job isn't a Mcwage job, the IRS has a catch-22 called the economic substance doctrine where they could argue that you were trying to fleece them in an audit or court proceeding.

62301881
There are actively managed funds that intentionally do stuff like this to get you tax breaks every year.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62302206
>>62301931
just buy WBTC lmao

62302609
>>62301884
You can deduct up to 3000 dollars a year in capital losses from your earned income

62302619
>>62301796
And then when you sell it at 100k at a real loss of 26k you have to pay taxes on fake capital gains of 11k.
Fucking idiot

62302625
Creative account hack

62302635
Illegal in my country.

62302646
>>62301905
in germany it's legal.

62302875
Yes and I do this but I'm in the UK so you have to wait 30 days to buy back (bed & breakfast rule), or you can swap it to another token like WBTC.

62303255
>>62301796
False. the us gov only accepts 3k in losses per year.

62303270
>>62301884
Wrong, I've taken the max deduction every single year for the past 5 years because I blow at trading

62303329
>>62301796
You can do this assuming the investment isn’t subject to wash sale rules. You can also swap between bitcoin ETFs that aren’t considered substantially identical. However doing this is going to make your cost basis much lower so when you sell in the future at a profit, you’ll have higher levels of taxable gains.

62303351
>>62303255
You can harvest unlimited losses to offset capital gains. If you sell stock A at a 5 mil profit and sell stock B at a 3 mil loss, you’ll only have 2 mil in taxable gains that year. The 3k limit only applies to lowering MAGI.

62303503
>people on /biz/ dont understand how capital gains taxes work
jesus christ guys get it together

62303568
>>62303270
based

62303876
>>62301905
There is no wash sales on crypto because its not a security, its classified as "property". That said its still not good to take losses. Making 10k is always better than losing 10k even if it lets you defer taxes on other earnings.

62303887
>>62303351
It has to be the same type of capital loss and gains though. You can't offset short term loss against long term gains.

62303938
>>62302190
correct, and you carry over anything not used to the next year

62304466
>>62303503
Why should we learn about a tax that we will never get to pay?

62304653
>>62303887
Look how confident this utter retard is. Lot of retards in here though excluding a couple anons.
It initially applies directly to the same type of capital gain/loss (short/long term). I’ll just give an example cause it’s harder to type out and explain it simply.

let’s say you
>realize a loss of 15k in a short term trade in Feb of 2026
>in April, you close another short term trade and realize a gain of $2500 in April.
>That puts you at -12500 on the year so far. Short for short, straightforward and as expected.
>You make no more sales of short term holdings for the rest of the year.
>in November, you sell a long term position and profit 9500 ltcg.
->since you have no more short term gains to cancel out, you can apply it to your long term gain
>you would end up net -3k on the year which you can then deduct from you MAGI
And ideally if you’re really scheming and planning, you obviously don’t want to realize a long term capital gain to cut down on a realized short term loss. It’s possible in incorrect about that last sentence. I won’t be smug like the brainlet I replied to. From my understanding you can’t just defer that long term realized gain

Here’s somethin I was thinking about as a possible plan I was contemplating (although I am aware about it carrying forward and I could use my “loss Bank” later in the future) , I have 19k in realized losses short from being a Stink marine already this year. I have long term precious metals, which are taxed at a rate of 28% on long term holds. I could realize my profits and sell some early gold bars that I got for low 2k as well as silver at 30, and use those short term losses to completely cancel out my realized profits in PMs 1:1.

62304666
>>62301796
>henlo IRS I losted $37,000 and want a refund
>yes I can hold

62304690
>>62304653
And obviously, same thing applies with long term realized losses, if you have no long term gains to cancel out for that year, it can be applied to the short term gains, but Im pretty certain it isn’t at a 1:1 like that PM based legal loophole. Maybe a percentage based off your cap gains rate and your tax bracket for the year if I recall

62304719
>>62302619
kek

62304792
>>62301796
..............

62305421
>>62301931
>wont be able to buy illicit goods anymore though
Sure you can, after 30 days.

62305550
>>62301820
It would be illegal if it was anything other than bitcoin.

62305573
>>62301796
Who the fuck is stupid enough to declare their crypto savings to the taxman?

62305634
>>62301820
Yeah, you sell the BTC to take the loss at the end of the year and then buy something like IBIT until the year rolls over then hold IBIT for year and repeat. If you really need the BTC you could just rebuy the BTC after Jan 1

62305652
>>62301796
That's the stated reason for Strategy's selling

62308022
>>62301796
that's a cool trick but unfortunately it also involves being retarded enough to buy at 126k

62308032
>>62305652
what gains do they have to offset though?

62308136
Good advice, except for the "buying it back" part.

item.title: you silly basterds think btc is down because of saylor man wait until normcattle hear about quantum fud on steroids >Goo… — 6 replies — 11.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
you silly basterds think btc is down because of saylor man wait until normcattle hear about quantum fud on steroids >Goo…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-03 09:39 UTC

OP

you silly basterds think btc is down because of saylor man
wait until normcattle hear about quantum fud on steroids

>Google cracked Shor's algorithm for secp256k1 (the curve securing btc keys)
>hid the details behind a ZK proof at US government request
>a french reverse-engineered it in two months
>cryptographic qday by 2032

[Submitted on 1 Jun 2026]
Optimized Point Addition Circuits for Elliptic Curve Discrete Logarithms
André Schrottenloher
https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02235

start sweating bitches

Early replies (first 3)

62306885
>>62306854
bip360. problem solved

62306909
also, he reverse-engineered it 10% better
and vibeGODS are VIBING it even better now
it's the ultimate humiliation

62307046
>>62306854
they said in that same paper that Mochimo already solved it, but also brought up QRL which is a scamcoin that Coinmarketcap CEO called out and had de-tracked due to inflated valuation. Both are supposedly quantum proof. I bought some MCM just in case. Qday is real.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62307066
>>62306854
>Saylor knew it
Is that why he sold only 0.0038% of this stash?

62308190
>>62306909
>vibeGODS
peasants who don't own AI
in a few years it will be:
legacy biomass that isn't AI

62308352
>>62306854
I'll explain cause somehow even among cryptofags there aren't any compsci people here...
"Cracking" the algorithm here actually means just halving the power of operations required to solve it. If it's 256 bit, then it's 2 to the power of 256. In the very best case scenario here, that goes to 2 to the power of 128 on a quantum processor. You will need many million times all of the current quantum processing power in the world right now to crack a single one in years, and these are extremely difficult to manufacture.

The real chokepoint for quantum computing for like 15 years now is that they just cannot make them large enough, not by a long shot. If they somehow had a breakthrough and were able to make a quantum processor 10 times as large (in terms of qubits) as the current world record, we'd still need to have like 3 more breakthroughs like that to even approach the possibility of craching a single 256 bit key.

item.title: RED ALERT RED ALERT RED ALERT — 6 replies — 8.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
RED ALERT RED ALERT RED ALERT

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-03 09:28 UTC

OP

>its not over until-ack...
ITS FUCKING ROYALLY OVER IN SO MANY FUCKING WAYS PEOPLE.

WAKE UP!!!!! NO SHIT BITCOIN IS DUMPING!!!!!

The Core Numbers
The Federal Debt: Publicly held debt reached $31.27 trillion.

The U.S. GDP: Total nominal economic output sat at $31.22 trillion.

The Current Ratio: The U.S. debt-to-GDP ratio currently stands at 100.2%.

This is the first time the national debt has eclipsed the U.S. economy since the immediate aftermath of World War II in 1946.

Early replies (first 3)

62306731
>>62306529
My dude, are you fucking retarded?

Your data is completely wrong, but even if it wasn't, high debt is bullish for BTC. That's exactly where it shines

62306740
>>62306731
Pretty sure you're the retard here by a country mile! kek https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/americas-finance-guide/national-debt/

62306775
>>62306731
You are retarded, anon.

>>62306740
Based and treasurypilled

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62306839
>>62306529
>since the immediate aftermath of World War II in 1946
remember what happened next? a decade of insane prosperity

62308325
>>62306740
Your source is showing the same as mine. Debt is over 39 trillion, gdp is 32 trillion. We went over 100% more than 4 years ago.

62308327
>>62308325
Sorry, typo, more than 14 years ago.

item.title: Actual insider here. I think this needs some clarification because Link marines have been waiting for almost 9 years now… — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Actual insider here. I think this needs some clarification because Link marines have been waiting for almost 9 years now…

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-06-03 10:08 UTC

OP

Actual insider here. I think this needs some clarification because Link marines have been waiting for almost 9 years now and have been extremely patient. I can't really tell you too much because Chainlink Labs employees actually lurk biz (and post here) so they might able to know who I am. There has been an unexpected temporary delay of DTCC adoption due to the upgraded off-chain computation protocols (OCR 2.0) that had an unforeseen malfunction. However, the DONs seem to be fully online therefore the problem will be resolved quite rapidly.

In regards to SWIFT they actually seem to be fully operational by June. The main problem is that the network across EVM and non-EVM chains hasn't been fully tested to maximum capacity therefore releasing a product in such a short time would be quite a gamble for us. I'm confident enough that we'll be able to maintain a standardized interface to control the minimization protocol for the off-chain computation of the blockchain thus rendering the entire Chainlink ecosystem fully secured. At this rate I can only tell you that DTCC and SWIFT will be operational by the end of June, that's a certainty.

Strap yourselves in
It’s actually happening

Early replies (first 3)

62308409
AI post

62308412
>>62308361
>Actual insider here
Prove it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire? — 69 replies — 56.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire?

Open thread • Replies: 69 • Updated: 2026-06-03 08:59 UTC

OP

What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire? Assuming one lives in USA

Early replies (first 3)

62299698
>>62299690
Just do nothing and wait, we will all be billionaires within the next decade

62299702
>Assuming one lives in USA
the first step is to get the fuck out of that dangerous brown shithole and go to Europe

62299711
>>62299690
Inheritance

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62304411
>>62302523
>invest 1 million a month in index funds, after only one month you’ll have 1 million (enough to retire)

62304496
>>62302537
Get a $96k per year remote job, and slum it in the deep south for 20 years. You might be able to get away living off only $48k a year. Or find the money some other way, I'm just telling you whats required.
>>62304411
You're forgetting about the magic of compound interest friend. $4k a month is only $960k after 20 years, coumpound interet is giving you a free million dollars.

62304513
buy link and delude yourself into believing $1k eoy. there are lots of (essentially) millionaires like this on this board

62305112
>>62299690
>multimillionaire
>2026
Was born in a rich family.
None others.

62305121
>>62305112
Not meaning to move the goal post, but this is assuming one does not have outside help starting from $0

62305139
>>62302523
>>62299716
> low-cost index funds
Only worked in boomer casino due to FED bank inflation that inflated all assets,
including stocks.
Won't work in the next 20 years.
Be worried that the casino would drop 30-50%, and you'd lose this much and won't recover.

62305158
>>62305121
Buddy.
Let's assume you have $1000.
You need to go 1000x for it to be $1 mil.
10000x to have 10 mil.
The odds are as low as winning a lottery.
I'd start at getting a job where you can earn enough so that you can have enough disposable income to invest.

62305193
>>62305158
Already got a job and saved up 160k in a few years living frugally. I can confirm this is the most sure way to 6 figs, but going from 160k to 2m+ is going to take a lifetime at my current rate. Certainly not low stress either.

62306348
>>62305139
Nothing is ever certain, but the stock market has an average return of 10% long term, everyone and their grandmother keeps their money in it. There would literally be mass suicides if it dropped 30%, so its in the best interest of the fed and every other investor to keep it growing. Conspiracy theories of its demise have existed for decades, and its still here.

62306361
>>62305193
Yeah 20 years if you dont add any more money, but youre obviously not going to do that. Just keep saving and investing and making good choices and you'll be financially independent in probaby 10 years.

62306493
>>62304411
what's the catch?

62306609
>>62306348
>There would literally be mass suicides if it dropped 30%
you mean the gfc?

62306632
>>62299690
picking a super bowl winner, not turning on the game and just playing vidya and then wake up the next morning forgetting you even placed the bet.

62306693
>>62299690
depends on iq
for me its algorithmic trading with rebalancing mag 7 and kabsa

62306705
>>62299690
0dtes

62307416
>>62300255
Dumb fucking pajeet nigger monkey.

62307546
>>62299690
get a high paying job at a hedge fund then index meme i thought this was obvious

62307548
>>62307416
lmaooo true

62307616
Not stress-free but it involves buying a fat stack of toad.

62307657
>>62299690
Depends how old you are
But for people under 30, just have a high savings rate (spend as little as possible on cars and eating/drinking as a form of entertainment, they're probably the biggest budget killers for most people), take advantage of every tax advantaged account (401k, hsa, etc.) available to you, and you'll probably hit millionaire status in 15-30 years, depending on what your savings rate was, which is of course heavily determined by income. But any midwit making 75-100k could do this.

62307680
>>62299690
you can actually go on google and ask how most millionaires made their money by percentage.

protip: Nobody on /biz/ knows what they're talking about.

second protip: Because nobody here knows how other people get rich, you can safely bet nobody here is actually rich.

62307719
>>62307680
Old news. Most millionaires today bought a decent house ten years ago.
A $250k house in 2016 is now worth over a million almost everywhere in the US.

It's literally that simple. A million just ain't what it used to be (because of inflation and the federal reserve scam).

62307733
>>62307719
notice that owning a house does not appear in the list

your headcanon is something I started because I made half a million buying a house.

but the other half is my business.

the average american has a $400k house and about $1000 cash, making them NOT a millionaire. Also paid off houses are pretty fucking rare.

62307829
>>62307733
>notice that owning a house does not appear in the list
Yeah my point was explicitly that the list is wrong and most people are paper millionaires because of housing equity and not having a reason to move in the last decade. Not because they all go around starting businesses and becoming corporate executives.

It's like how those lists of the richest people in the world always have a little asterisk: they explicitly exclude royalty and nobility. Which is 80% of the world's wealthiest people.

62307847
>>62307829
>Yeah my point was explicitly that the list is wrong
weird how you're right and everyone that studies wealth is wrong.

you should find a way to cash in on your superior ass-knowledge

62307850
>>62299690
Buy chain*nk <$9

62307874
>>62307847
>people who study wealth
lmao
Okay kid

62308115
>>62307874
yes because a bunch of literal retards and schizophrenics on 4chan spouting what they FEEL are better than actual surveys and statistics

Tell me how you FEEL people get rich, dumbass.

then go on google and learn how people actually get rich if you have the balls to face reality.

62308126
>>62299690
Depends on what you see as stress

Become a doctor or lawyer, law maker, convert to Judaism etc.

62308257
>>62299690
>What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire?
Stay single. Pussy is your single biggest obstacle to wealth & happiness.
